summary: Localization: the languages chosen at create time, generated ARB/resource files, an in-app language picker, and Accept-Language handling on the API.
userSteps: The app's languages were fixed at create time and live in `app/lib/l10n/app_<code>.arb` and `api/src/Api/Resources/Localization/Messages[.<code>].resx`; English is the fallback for both sides.
Add or change a string by editing every `app_<code>.arb`, then rebuild — `flutter gen-l10n` runs on each build and regenerates `AppL10n` under `app/lib/l10n/gen/`.
The API answers in the language the client asks for via the `Accept-Language` header; the Flutter client sends it automatically for the selected locale.

wiring.insert:
locale: context.watch<LocaleCubit>().state,
      localizationsDelegates: AppL10nSupport.delegates,
      supportedLocales: AppL10nSupport.supportedLocales,
      onGenerateTitle: (context) => AppL10n.of(context).appTitle,
